spec_match.rationale: The adapter types and workloads are fully simulated in client JS rather than requesting a real WebGPU adapter and matching the user's live adapterType.

interactive.rationale: The workload button switches the active card and shows the code snippets/decisions, which is helpful and interactive.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the main page blurb, highlighting the discrete vs integrated vs CPU distinction.

edge_cases.rationale: Correctly demonstrates powerPreference and forceFallbackAdapter request options.

references.rationale: Cites the ChromeStatus entry and links back to features and basic concepts.

design_system.rationale: Follows standard design system layout and CSS variables.

openQuestions.title: Incorporate live WebGPU adapter inspection
openQuestions.detail: The adapter-selection page is currently simulated. To satisfy the genuinely interactive requirements, the page should execute navigator.gpu.requestAdapter() on load and highlight the user's actual adapter type (discrete, integrated, or CPU fallback) in the grid, fallback gracefully if WebGPU is missing.

summary: An interactive cards-based workload choice simulator, explaining why the new enum is superior, which would benefit from real GPU capability probing.
